description: Set generalized point loads for all nodes in the range. Generalized point loads consist of an applied force and an applied moment. Pressure loading can be applied to shell surfaces using the commands structure shell apply , structure geogrid apply , or structure liner apply . Distributed loads can be applied to beam and pile surfaces using the command structure beam apply or structure pile apply . The following keywords are available to define generalized point loads.

6.0.description: Set generalized point loads for all nodes in the range. Generalized point loads consist of an applied force and an applied moment. Pressure loading can be applied to shell surfaces using the commands structure shell apply, structure geogrid apply, or structure liner apply. Distributed loads can be applied to beam and pile surfaces using the command structure beam apply or structure pile apply. The following keywords are available to define generalized point loads.

keywords.name: force
keywords.description: applied force vector (in terms of system defined by the system keyword). You can use one of the following keywords to modify how v is applied to the node:

keywords.name: force-add
keywords.description: the vector v is added to the existing applied force vector at that node

keywords.name: force-multiply
keywords.description: the vector v is multiplied to the existing applied force vector at that node in a component by component multiplication

keywords.name: force-edge
keywords.description: by default, these forces will overwrite the previous applied forces at the node; however, the add keyword can be given to add them to those currently present. Note that if this changes the system they were applied under, the previous forces will not be corrected for the system change.

keywords.name: moment
keywords.description: applied moment vector (in terms of system defined by the system keyword). The following keywords can be used to modify how v is applied to the node:

keywords.name: moment-add
keywords.description: the vector v is added to the existing applied moment vector at that node

keywords.name: moment-multiply
keywords.description: the vector v is multiplied to the existing applied moment vector at that node in a component by component multiplication

keywords.name: remove
keywords.description: an applied condition remains in effect until it is removed with this command. The following keywords are available to choose which applied condition is removed:

keywords.name: remove-force
keywords.description: removes any applied force condition on the node

keywords.name: remove-moment
keywords.description: removes any applied moment condition on the node

keywords.name: system
keywords.description: set the coordinate system in which the force and moment vectors are expressed. The default system is global. If the system is set to local, then in large-strain mode, the force and moment vectors will remain aligned with the local system as it rotates.

7.0.description: Set generalized point loads for all nodes in the range. Generalized point loads consist of an applied force and an applied moment. Pressure loading can be applied to shell surfaces using the commands structure shell apply, structure geogrid apply, or structure liner apply. Distributed loads can be applied to beam and pile surfaces using the command structure beam apply or structure pile apply. The following keywords are available to define generalized point loads.

keywords.name: force
keywords.description: applied force vector (in terms of system defined by the system keyword). You can use one of the following keywords to modify how v is applied to the node:

keywords.name: force-add
keywords.description: the vector v is added to the existing applied force vector at that node

keywords.name: force-multiply
keywords.description: the vector v is multiplied to the existing applied force vector at that node in a component by component multiplication

keywords.name: force-edge
keywords.description: by default, these forces will overwrite the previous applied forces at the node; however, the add keyword can be given to add them to those currently present. Note that if this changes the system they were applied under, the previous forces will not be corrected for the system change.

keywords.name: moment
keywords.description: applied moment vector (in terms of system defined by the system keyword). The following keywords can be used to modify how v is applied to the node:

keywords.name: moment-add
keywords.description: the vector v is added to the existing applied moment vector at that node

keywords.name: moment-multiply
keywords.description: the vector v is multiplied to the existing applied moment vector at that node in a component by component multiplication

keywords.name: remove
keywords.description: an applied condition remains in effect until it is removed with this command. The following keywords are available to choose which applied condition is removed:

keywords.name: remove-force
keywords.description: removes any applied force condition on the node

keywords.name: remove-moment
keywords.description: removes any applied moment condition on the node

keywords.name: system
keywords.description: set the coordinate system in which the force and moment vectors are expressed. The default system is global. If the system is set to local, then in large-strain mode, the force and moment vectors will remain aligned with the local system as it rotates.

keywords.name: force
keywords.description: Apply a force vector v to nodes in the range. Optional sub-keywords add or multiply modify how the applied force combines with any existing applied force.

keywords.name: force-add
keywords.description: Add the new force vector to the existing applied force on each node (instead of replacing).

keywords.name: force-multiply
keywords.description: Multiply the existing applied force on each node by the supplied force vector component-wise.

keywords.name: force-edge
keywords.description: Apply a force-per-unit-length vector v along the edge between two nodes. Same add/multiply modifiers as `force`.

keywords.name: moment
keywords.description: Apply a moment vector v to nodes in the range. Optional add/multiply modifiers as for `force`.

keywords.name: moment-add
keywords.description: Add the new moment vector to the existing applied moment.

keywords.name: moment-multiply
keywords.description: Multiply the existing applied moment by the supplied vector component-wise.

keywords.name: remove
keywords.description: Remove an existing applied force/moment from nodes in the range. Sub-keywords: force, moment.

keywords.name: remove-force
keywords.description: Remove the applied force from nodes in the range.

keywords.name: remove-moment
keywords.description: Remove the applied moment from nodes in the range.

keywords.name: system
keywords.description: Set the coordinate system in which the applied force/moment is interpreted. Sub-keywords: local, global.

keywords.name: system-local
keywords.description: Applied force/moment is interpreted in the node-local coordinate system.

keywords.name: system-global
keywords.description: Applied force/moment is interpreted in the global coordinate system.

keywords.name: range
keywords.description: Range block selecting which nodes the apply condition is applied to.
